hook.text: ALTER TABLE on a billion-row table locks it for minutes. Users see errors. Zero-downtime migrations avoid that.

buildup.text: The trick: add the new column as nullable, backfill data in batches, then add the constraint.

discovery.text: Rename a column? Add the new one, dual-write, migrate reads, drop the old one. Four steps, no downtime.

twist.text: Some databases support non-blocking DDL (Postgres mostly does). Others lock the table during ALTER.

climax.text: Tools like gh-ost and pt-online-schema-change do live migrations for MySQL without locking.

punchline.text: Migrate in steps. Never lock a live table for minutes.

quiz.question: Why should you add new columns as nullable first during a live migration?
options: It avoids locking the table and lets you backfill data in safe batches
Nullable columns use less disk space
The database requires all new columns to be nullable
